title: Suez Canal Blocked Again by Container Ship
subtitle: Ever Forward runs aground, triggering global supply chain chaos

ripples.headline: Oil prices surge 15% as tankers queue in Red Sea
European car factories halt production amid parts shortage
Amazon Prime delivery delays spread across US East Coast
Coffee bean shortage drives Starbucks prices up 8%
Wedding planners scramble as flower imports delayed
Local bookstore raises prices on imported novels
Sarah's birthday gift arrives late, burrito costs $18

symphony_arc.opening: A massive container ship runs aground in the Suez Canal, instantly creating a maritime traffic jam that will ripple across continents.
symphony_arc.development: Global supply chains, already fragile from previous disruptions, begin to fracture as oil tankers queue and factories halt production.
symphony_arc.climax: The blockage creates cascading shortages from car parts to coffee beans, driving inflation and touching every corner of the economy.
symphony_arc.resolution: While the ship is eventually freed, the butterfly effect continues for weeks, culminating in overpriced burritos and delayed birthday presents.

choices: Invest in alternative shipping routes immediately
Stockpile essential goods before prices surge
Short shipping companies, long oil futures
